ambiguities.aspect: Sale vs secured borrowing treatment of discounted note
ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: Convention marks 'discounted at the bank' as explicitly ambiguous, BUT the transaction text states 'Management has determined that this is a collateralized borrowing' — this is a definitive stated determination.
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: IFRS 9 derecognition depends on risk/reward transfer assessment, BUT management has explicitly determined collateralized borrowing treatment applies.

ambiguities.aspect: Calculation of discount charges and net proceeds
ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: Discount = face value × discount rate × (remaining days / 365); remaining days = 90 - 50 = 40 days; discount = $100,000 × 15% × (40/365) = $1,643.84; net proceeds = $100,000 - $1,643.84 = $98,356.16

ambiguities.aspect: Initial receipt of note from customer
ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: Non-interest-bearing note received as 'settlement of trade receivable' — the note replaces the receivable at face value (no time-value adjustment mentioned or implied for short-term trade settlement).
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: IFRS 9: short-term receivables may be measured at invoice amount if discounting effect is immaterial (practical expedient).

best_attempt.reason: Collateralized borrowing: note remains on books, cash received equals face value less discount charges for 40 remaining days at 15% annual rate
Transaction describes two events: (1) initial receipt of note from customer, (2) discounting at bank; the question asks about the discounting event only
